L. 86–249, § 1473 Stat. 483Sept. 9, 1959In subsection (a), the text of 40:612(2) and 612a(1) is omitted because the complete name of the Administrator of General Services is used the first time the term appears in a section. In clause (5)(A), the words “mixed-ownership Government corporation” are substituted for “mixed ownership corporation” for consistency with 31:9101. In clause (5)(B) and (C), the words “from time to time hereafter” are omitted as unnecessary. In clause (6), the words “territories and” are added for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.In subsection (b), the text of 40:613(1)–(3) is omitted as obsolete. The reference is to section 241(g) of the Immigration and Nationality Act rather than to section 242(c) to reflect the amendment of sections 241 and 242 by sections 305(a)(3) and 306(a)(2) of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 (, , 3009–607).Public Law 104–208, div. The reference is to section 241(g) of the Immigration and Nationality Act rather than to section 242(c) to reflect the amendment of sections 241 and 242 by sections 305(a)(3) and 306(a)(2) of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 (, , 3009–607).Public Law 104–208, div. children.label: A
children.text: preliminary planning, engineering, architectural, legal, fiscal, and economic investigations and studies, surveys, designs, plans, working drawings, specifications, procedures, and other similar actions necessary for the alteration of a public building; and

children.label: B
children.text: repairing, remodeling, improving, or extending, or other changes in, a public building.

children.heading: .—Construct
children.label: 2 .—Construct
children.text: The term “construct” includes preliminary planning, engineering, architectural, legal, fiscal, and economic investigations and studies, surveys, designs, plans, working drawings, specifications, procedures, and other similar actions necessary for the construction of a public building.

children.label: A
children.text: any wholly owned Government corporation;

children.label: B
children.text: the Central-Bank for Cooperatives and the regional banks for cooperatives;

children.label: C
children.text: federal land banks;

children.label: D
children.text: federal intermediate credit banks;

children.label: E
children.text: the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation; and

children.label: F
children.text: the Government National Mortgage Association.

children.heading: .—Federal agency
children.label: 4 .—Federal agency
children.text: The term “federal agency” means an executive agency or an establishment in the legislative or judicial branch of the Government (except the Senate, the House of Representatives, and the Architect of the Capitol and any activities under the direction of the Architect).

children.label: A
children.text: means a building, whether for single or multitenant occupancy, and its grounds, approaches, and appurtenances, which is generally suitable for use as office or storage space or both by one or more federal agencies or mixed-ownership Government corporations;

children.label: i
children.text: federal office buildings;

children.label: ii
children.text: post offices;

children.label: iii
children.text: customhouses;

children.label: iv
children.text: courthouses;

children.label: v
children.text: appraisers stores;

children.label: vi
children.text: border inspection facilities;

children.label: vii
children.text: warehouses;

children.label: viii
children.text: record centers;

children.label: ix
children.text: relocation facilities;

children.label: x
children.text: telecommuting centers;

children.label: xi
children.text: similar federal facilities; and

children.label: xii
children.text: any other buildings or construction projects the inclusion of which the President considers to be justified in the public interest; but

children.label: i
children.text: that is on the public domain (including that reserved for national forests and other purposes);

children.label: ii
children.text: that is on property of the Government in foreign countries;

children.label: iii
children.text: that is on Indian and native Eskimo property held in trust by the Government;

children.label: iv
children.text: that is on land used in connection with federal programs for agricultural, recreational, and conservation purposes, including research in connection with the programs;

children.label: v
children.text: that is on or used in connection with river, harbor, flood control, reclamation or power projects, for chemical manufacturing or development projects, or for nuclear production, research, or development projects;

children.label: vi
children.text: that is on or used in connection with housing and residential projects;

children.label: vii
children.text: that is on military installations (including any fort, camp, post, naval training station, airfield, proving ground, military supply depot, military school, or any similar facility of the Department of Defense);

children.label: viii
children.text: that is on installations of the Department of Veterans Affairs used for hospital or domiciliary purposes; or

children.label: ix
children.text: the exclusion of which the President considers to be justified in the public interest.

children.heading: .—United states
children.label: 6 .—United states
children.text: The term “United States” includes the States of the United States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, and the territories and possessions of the United States.

children.heading: .—Nonapplication
children.label: b .—Nonapplication
children.text: This chapter does not apply to the construction of any public building to which section 241(g) of the Immigration and Nationality Act () or () applies.8 U.S.C. 1231(g)section 1 of the Act of June 26, 193019 U.S.C. 68
